question: What is swarm's base calibration on real-world predictions at the 3-day mark?
expect: Directional accuracy <50% (worse than coin flip). Swarm calibration overconfident on bear thesis.
actual: 53.3% directional accuracy (8/15). Brier score 0.246 vs 0.25 random baseline. Bear predictions 0/4 correct. Bull predictions 5/8 correct. Neutral 2/2 correct. Swarm has slight bull-detection ability but bear-detection is zero.
diff: Accuracy slightly BETTER than expected (53.3% vs <50%) but Brier CONFIRMS no skill (0.246 ≈ 0.25). Bear overconfidence CONFIRMED: all 4 bear predictions wrong. Key surprise: neutrals were 100% correct — swarm is better at identifying range-bound assets than directional moves.
context: Trump TACO rally: announced Iran peace talks, paused strikes. SPY +1.3%, oil -4.6%, GLD -4.9%. Risk-on day. Many predictions made during peak fear (S499, Iran war week). This is exactly the key_risk scenario most bear predictions identified.

findings.worst_prediction: PRED-0004 GLD BULL: -4.9% (completely wrong direction, high confidence)
findings.best_prediction: PRED-0013 EEM BULL: +2.9% (correct direction, on track to target)
findings.key_insight: Swarm predicted its own failure mode: every bear prediction listed 'ceasefire/de-escalation' as key_risk. The key_risk materialized and all bear predictions failed. This is GOOD calibration of risks even though directional prediction failed.
findings.structural_bias: Bear thesis dominated by narrative (stagflation, CAPE, oil shock) while ignoring mean-reversion and political dynamics (Trump incentive to de-escalate). Narrative-driven predictions are fragile to single-event reversals.
